2026-03-18T07:40:56.2938052Z Agent: 
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2941032Z Agent: Now let me check the API surface issues. Looking at `arm-previewalertrule-models-node.api.md`, I'm seeing that `KnownAlertSeverity` has some numeric string values like `_0`, `_1`, `_2`.  These underscore-prefixed enum values violate the naming guidelines—the spec should use `@clientName` to provide better names instead of relying on these underscore prefixes. I'm also checking for other common issues like `AutoGenerated` suffixes or `_N` patterns on types, but those don't appear to be present here.
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2944517Z Agent: 
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2946817Z Agent: Looking at the API files, the main operations like `previewAlertRule` return proper types without `unknown` or `void`, and the client structure looks reasonable overall. Now I need to dig deeper into the `KnownAlertSeverity` issue to see if there are similar naming problems there.
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2948910Z Agent: 
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2951376Z Agent: The enum values are prefixed with underscores because the underlying numeric values (0-4) can't start a TypeScript identifier, so the generator adds `_` as a workaround. This violates the naming guidelines and should be flagged as something that needs better handling on the spec side, potentially using `@clientName` for cleaner names.

2026-03-18T07:40:56.2960943Z Agent: 1. **Version consistency**: ✅
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2961282Z Agent:    - `package.json`: `1.0.0-beta.1`
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2961804Z Agent:    - `CHANGELOG.md`: `1.0.0-beta.1 (2026-03-18)`

2026-03-18T07:40:56.2962996Z Agent:    - All consistent ✅
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2963258Z Agent: 
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2963513Z Agent: 2. **No alpha versions**: ✅
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2963755Z Agent: 
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2964229Z Agent: 3. **First CHANGELOG entry**: It's the first release, so we ignore content. The version is `1.0.0-beta.1`. ✅
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2964745Z Agent:    
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2965344Z Agent: 4. **API version alignment**: The API version `2025-07-01-preview` matches the preview status of the package version `1.0.0-beta.1`. ✅
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2965975Z Agent: 
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2968281Z Agent: 5. **`KnownAlertSeverity` enum values**: The enum has underscore-prefixed names like `_0`, `_1`, `_2`, `_3`, `_4`, which violates the guideline against using `_` prefixes for enum names—this suggests the spec needs better naming, and we should recommend using `@clientName` in TypeSpec to improve it.
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2969483Z Agent: 
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2969966Z Agent: 6. **Breaking changes**: Since this is the first release, there are no breaking changes to worry about. ✅
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2970449Z Agent: 
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2971289Z Agent: 7. **Type safety**: Return types avoid `unknown` (with `ErrorAdditionalInfo.info` being `any`, which is acceptable), and operations like `previewAlertRule` properly return typed promises instead of `void`. ✅
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2972111Z Agent: 
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2972385Z Agent: 8. **Exports**: All public symbols are properly exported.
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2972715Z Agent: 
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2973086Z Agent: 9. **Forgotten exports**: No warnings about missing exports in the API report. ✅
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2973481Z Agent: 
2026-03-18T07:40:56.2974647Z Agent: So the main issue I'm finding is the naming of the `KnownAlertSeverity` enum values—they're prefixed with underscores (`_0`, `_1`, `_2`, `_3`, `_4`) when they should be named without them (`Sev0`, `Sev1`, etc.). This is a design issue in the TypeSpec definition that needs to be fixed at the source.

file\"},{\"filename\":\"sdk/previewalertrule/arm-previewalertrule/README.md\",\"status\":\"added\",\"additions\":113,\"changes\":113,\"patch\":\"@@ -0,0 +1,113 @@\\n+# Azure PreviewAlertRuleManagement client library for JavaScript\\n+\\n+This package contains an isomorphic SDK (runs both in Node.js and in browsers) for Azure PreviewAlertRuleManagement client.\\n+\\n+The Preview Alert Rule API provides the ability to retrieve the results of a simulated historical execution of an alert rule\\n+\\n+Key links:\\n+\\n+- [Source code](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/tree/main/sdk/previewalertrule/arm-previewalertrule)\\n+- [Package (NPM)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@azure/arm-previewalertrule)\\n+- [API reference documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/javascript/api/@azure/arm-previewalertrule?view=azure-node-preview)\\n+- [Samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/tree/main/sdk/previewalertrule/arm-previewalertrule/samples)\\n+\\n+## Getting started\\n+\\n+### Currently supported environments\\n+\\n+- [LTS versions of Node.js](https://github.com/nodejs/release#release-schedule)\\n+- Latest versions of Safari, Chrome, Edge and Firefox.\\n+\\n+See our [support policy](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/blob/main/SUPPORT.md) for more details.\\n+\\n+### Prerequisites\\n+\\n+- An [Azure subscription][azure_sub].\\n+\\n+### Install the `@azure/arm-previewalertrule` package\\n+\\n+Install the Azure PreviewAlertRuleManagement client library for JavaScript with `npm`:\\n+\\n+```bash\\n+npm install @azure/arm-previewalertrule\\n+```\\n+\\n+### Create and authenticate a `PreviewAlertRuleManagementClient`\\n+\\n+To create a client object to access the Azure PreviewAlertRuleManagement API, you will need the `endpoint` of your Azure PreviewAlertRuleManagement resource and a `credential`. The Azure PreviewAlertRuleManagement client can use Azure Active Directory credentials to authenticate.\\n+You can find the endpoint for your Azure PreviewAlertRuleManagement resource in the [Azure Portal][azure_portal].\\n+\\n+You can authenticate with Azure Active Directory using a credential from the [@azure/identity][azure_identity] library or [an existing AAD Token](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/blob/main/sdk/identity/identity/samples/AzureIdentityExamples.md#authenticating-with-a-pre-fetched-access-token).\\n+\\n+To use the [DefaultAzureCredential][defaultazurecredential] provider shown below, or other credential providers provided with the Azure SDK, please install the `@azure/identity` package:\\n+\\n+```bash\\n+npm install @azure/identity\\n+```\\n+\\n+You will also need to **register a new AAD application and grant access to Azure PreviewAlertRuleManagement** by assigning the suitable role to your service principal (note: roles such as `\\\"Owner\\\"` will not grant the necessary permissions).\\n+\\n+For more information about how to create an Azure AD Application check out [this guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/howto-create-service-principal-portal).\\n+\\n+Using Node.js and Node-like environments, you can use the `DefaultAzureCredential` class to authenticate the client.\\n+\\n+```ts snippet:ReadmeSampleCreateClient_Node\\n+import { PreviewAlertRuleManagementClient } from \\\"@azure/arm-previewalertrule\\\";\\n+import { DefaultAzureCredential } from \\\"@azure/identity\\\";\\n+\\n+const client = new PreviewAlertRuleManagementClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n+```\\n+\\n+For browser environments, use the `InteractiveBrowserCredential` from the `@azure/identity` package to authenticate.\\n+\\n+```ts snippet:ReadmeSampleCreateClient_Browser\\n+import { InteractiveBrowserCredential } from \\\"@azure/identity\\\";\\n+import { PreviewAlertRuleManagementClient } from \\\"@azure/arm-previewalertrule\\\";\\n+\\n+const credential = new InteractiveBrowserCredential({\\n+  tenantId: \\\"\\u003cYOUR_TE
2026-03-18T07:40:56.5024008Z ...
